claim: Scaling RL to 1000+ layers unlocks exponential performance gains through self-supervised learning (representation learning) rather than value-based RL, which collapses when deep.
actionable: For deep RL agents, use self-supervised representation learning (contrastive objectives) instead of value regression; test on robotics tasks where this shows multiplicative gains.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Residual connections, layer normalization, and shift from regression (Q-values) to classification (trajectory membership) are critical architectural components; depth alone doesn't work.
actionable: When scaling RL depth, combine residual connections, layer norm, and classification-based objectives; don't expect naive depth scaling to improve performance.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Scaling depth is more parameter-efficient than width (linear vs quadratic parameter growth); 64-128 layers achieves near-optimal performance; extreme 1000-layer networks may be research artifacts.
actionable: For deployment, use 64-128 layer networks for parameter efficiency; reserve 1000-layer models for research or extremely high-throughput environments.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Performance doesn't scale monotonically; there are critical depths (50M+ transitions) where performance 'multiplies' once architectural components align—this phase transition requires abundant data.
actionable: Plan for phase transitions in scaling; expect polynomial improvements below critical depth, exponential above; ensure data collection infrastructure before deep scaling.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: JAX + GPU-accelerated environments (Jax GCRL) enable collecting hundreds of millions of transitions in hours, unlocking data abundance that makes deep scaling viable.
actionable: Use JAX and GPU-accelerated simulators for RL research; parallelize trajectory collection across environments to unlock the data needed for deep scaling.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: This isn't 'make networks bigger'—the core insight is shifting RL objectives from reward maximization to self-supervised representation learning; the code doesn't maximize rewards.
actionable: Reframe deep RL as deep representation learning, not deep reward modeling; experiment with self-supervised objectives (contrastive learning) alongside traditional RL.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Scaling depth unlocks scaling of batch size; traditional RL doesn't benefit from large batch sizes because networks are too shallow to leverage them.
actionable: When scaling RL, increase depth first to unlock batch size benefits; expect batch size scaling only after crossing critical depth thresholds.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Goal-conditional RL with deep networks could scale robotics training without imitation learning or massive human supervision, unlike current approaches requiring curated datasets.
actionable: Invest in goal-conditional RL for robotics as alternative to imitation learning; scale with self-supervised objectives rather than human demonstrations.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: Deep teacher, shallow student (distillation) is a promising future direction for deploying trained RL policies while maintaining inference efficiency.
actionable: Train deep RL agents, then distill to shallow networks for deployment; this balances frontier capability with practical latency requirements.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton

claim: All experiments ran on a single H100 80GB GPU, making this research accessible; distributed training could push scaling further.
actionable: Attempt deep RL experiments on single H100 GPU; know that results represent conservative estimates of what's possible with more compute.
source_title: 1000 Layer Networks for Self-Supervised RL — Kevin Wang et al, Princeton
